title: Karaivetti Bird Sanctuary
tagline: Karaivetti Bird Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Karaivetti Bird Sanctuary is a 4.537-square-kilometre (1.752 sq mi) protected area and Ramsar site, located in the Ariyalur District of the state of Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: The Karaivetti Bird Sanctuary is a 4.537-square-kilometre (1.752 sq mi) protected area and Ramsar site, located in the Ariyalur District of the state of Tamil Nadu, India. The sanctuary is about 25 kilometres (16 mi) from Thanjavur and about 21 kilometres (13 mi) from Ariyalur. This freshwater lake is fed by Pullambadi, Kattalal canal and attracts thousands of birds every year. This lake was declared as a sanctuary in 1999 by the Government of Tamil Nadu and in 2024, it became a Ramsar site.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Karaivetti Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Karaivetti Bird Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Karaivetti Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (43 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (43 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Karaivetti Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Karaivetti Bird Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Karaivetti Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Karaivetti Bird Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Karaivetti Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Karaiyavetti, Keezhakavattankurichi, Ayansuthamalli, Elandakudam, Annimangalam.
